economy.macroSubtitle: Understanding the flow of money is essential for hegemonic achievements. In Victoria 3, your government rarely generates money out of thin air (except for Gold Minting). The economy is based on a closed cycle where Population, Jobs, Wages, and Consumption Expenditures dictate GDP growth.
economy.microSubtitle: Many achievements require becoming the largest producer of certain goods or building late-century armies. Knowing advanced production methods (PMs) by heart avoids vital resource shortages and bankruptcies in the interconnected market.
economy.pmTipDescription: Whenever you unlock a new technology that changes the production method, check the local tabs (F3 in-game). Changing everything at once without having the intermediate resources ready in your market will cause prices to skyrocket.

politics.title: Politics and Laws
politics.description: Politics in the game is a continuous cycle. The characteristics of your Population dictate which Interest Groups (IGs) hold power. In turn, these groups form the Government, propose and pass Laws. These Laws, when active, change the economic and social structure of the country, altering the conditions of the population base again through a powerful Feedback Loop.
politics.legitimacyDescription: Your government's Legitimacy dictates how quickly you can pass laws (Approval Phases). Putting IGs in government that hate each other (ideological incompatibility) or putting weak IGs without the support of the current Voting Law will cause your Legitimacy to plummet, stagnating the country and generating Radicals passively.

military.title: Military System
military.description: War never occurs in a vacuum. It begins in diplomacy and escalates to Mobilization, demanding enormous amounts of goods from your economy. The system is not based on micromanaging troops on the map, but on ensuring that your supply chain sustains the Battalions on the Frontlines before the country succumbs to War Exhaustion.
military.costDescription: Prolonged wars generate Dead and Wounded. Besides increasing War Exhaustion towards capitulation, these casualties trigger a terrible Feedback Loop: your Active Population (workers) disappears, creating dependents, and the nation's mourning massively increases the number of internal Radicals, which can trigger a civil war right after the end of the international war.

map.title: Campaign Routes and Combos
map.description: Many Victoria 3 achievements perfectly complement each other if executed in the same run. Select one of our Treasure Maps below to reveal a step-by-step flowchart on how to orchestrate impressive combos and achieve quick glory.

achievements.noResults: No achievements found with current filters.

guides.title: Core Nations & Strategies
guides.subtitle: These countries have the most exclusive achievements, thematic journals, and unique mechanics. Click any of them to read the complete Early Game and Late Game strategies to survive and thrive.

metadata.title: Victoria 3 — Achievements & Guides
metadata.description: Achievement tracker and strategy guides for Victoria 3
